title: The nuts and bolts of omics for the clinical allergist.
author_string: Virkud, Yamini V, Kelly, Rachel S, Wood, Caleb, Lasky-Su, Jessica A
journal_full: Annals of allergy, asthma & immunology : official publication of the American College of Allergy, Asthma, & Immunology
abstract: Omics, aka multi-omics, is an emerging area of research that is advancing the use of personalized medicine in clinical practice and is therefore relevant for the practicing allergist. We performed a literature search of a selection of scientific findings in omics and allergy, including variants that may be important to allergy outcomes in the genome, transcriptome, metabolome, microbiome, epigenome, and exposome, among others. Basic science papers and review articles. The use of multi-omic data in clinical practice is changing how clinicians treat their patients whereby more personalized approaches are becoming standard in medical practice and has the potential to transform the treatment of allergies. Multi-omic data are relevant and will become increasingly important for the clinical allergist.
